prompt:
A HarborClass Booking & Dispatch system with main features including multi-role scheduling, creator content management, and an offline notification center for education and training organizations that run in-person sessions. Users include Students, Teachers, Couriers (for physical material delivery between sites), and Administrators. The English UI is rendered with Go Templ templates and provides a role-aware home dashboard: Students can browse available sessions, create a booking, view an order timeline, request reschedules or cancellations, and submit refund requests that move into review; they also manage message preferences with one-click unsubscribe per message category. Teachers maintain a creator profile, pin featured works, and use a content console to bulk edit, unpublish, or delete class materials and posts, plus see lightweight analytics such as 7/30/90-day views, likes+favorites, and follower growth. Administrators manage organization/class membership, configure who can set check-in rules, who can export data, and who can approve refunds or schedule exceptions. Dispatchers assign Couriers to delivery-type orders using configurable strategies such as distance-first, rating-first, or load-balanced, with restricted time windows (for example, no pickups after 8:00 PM) and blacklisted zones defined at the facility level; the UI gives immediate feedback for conflicts (double-booked time slots, ineligible courier, blocked zone) and shows a visible order state badge from created through completion or rollback.

The backend uses Gin to implement a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the Templ-based server-rendered pages and incremental form actions, with PostgreSQL as the system of record. Bookings and deliveries share a state-machine-driven order workflow with strict transition rules (for example, a confirmed order can reschedule up to 2 times, cancellations within 24 hours require teacher approval, and refund requests must be filed within 7 days of completion); each order has a human-readable order number formatted like HC-03282026-000742 and includes reconciliation fields for internal payment status (unpaid/waived/cash-recorded/refund-pending/refunded) without any online payment dependency. The notification & messaging delivery center runs locally: admins manage templates and subscriptions, the system writes delivery attempts to an audit table, enforces send rate limits (max 3 reminders per order per day), retries failed sends up to 5 times with exponential backoff, and respects unsubscribe flags. Audit logs are tamper-evident by hashing each log entry with the previous hash, recording key actions such as upload/download/share/permission changes/approvals/delete/rollback and all order operations; admins can search by user, time range, or resource and export to a local file. Multi-role authorization is enforced with org-class-member scope checks, and sensitive fields (passwords, PII like phone numbers) are stored encrypted at rest with masking in views. System operations and observability include client/server logs, request metrics, crash reports, API health checks, and offline alerting via an admin console, plus canary and forced-upgrade policies for managed devices without relying on any third-party services.
database: PostgreSQL (system of record, tamper-evident audit logs)
